IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED THAT FURTHER RESPONSES DUE 07/29/11. COURT ALSO SIGNED ORDER RE PLAINTIFFS DEMURRER TO DEFENDANTS ANSWER TO COMPLAINT RE HEARING ON 05/03/11. WHILE COURT RECONSIDERED THE SANCTION AMOUNT BASED ON ARGUMENTS PROFERRED BY DEFENSE COUNSEL, SUCH ARGUMENTS DID NOT APPLY TO MANY OF THE DISPUTED ITEMS WHICH CALLED FOR INFORMATION THAT DEFENDANTS WOULD HAVE LONG BEFORE THE REQUESTS AND COULD HAVE BEEN PROVIDED OR USED TO ADMIT OR DENY REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS. Off Calendar May-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph Bravo, Esq., from Bravo & Margulies, 415-512-6700, for defendants and moving party
Gail M. McMahon, Esq., from McMahon Law Offices, 415-979-0555, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as modified.
DEFENDANT DAVY CHOU and LILLIAN FAN'S Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate As Void The Order Dated Jan-11-12 Entered By The Judge Pro Tempore made pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 473 is granted. Temporary Judge Mark Schreiber did not act without jurisdiction in issuing the order sought to be vacated. After sufficient notice to them that the motion would be heard by Temporary Judge Schreiber, defendants' failure to appear at the hearing was properly deemed consent to the motion being heard by Temporary Judge Schreiber. Nonetheless, the court finds that defendants' non-appearance was due solely to a calendaring error, wich is a sufficient basis for relief under section 473, and the order issued by Temporary Judge Schreiber will be vacated and plaintiffs' motion for discovery sanctions will be heard on June 14, 2012 at 9:30am before Judge Kahn provided that defendants deliver courtesy copies of all papers filed by the parties on the motion to department 302 no later than 4pm on May 29 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. If plaintiffs wish to seek monetary sanctions per that section, plaintiffs may file a motion no later than June 1 seeking such sanctions, defendants may file opposition papers no later than June 8, and the hearing will be on June 14 without the filing of any reply papers. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
